Ad Text:
Company Description

Bapcor Ltd is the leading automotive aftermarket provider of accessories, equipment and service. We host one of the widest ranges of car parts in the world, with brands including Burson, Autobarn, Midas, Truckline, Premier Auto Trade and Baxters.

We’re more than just automotive — we’re a dynamic, purpose-led organisation powered by people. Our Melbourne-based corporate team plays a critical role in shaping the strategy and success of the Bapcor Group.

In joining our team, you’ll be welcomed into a supportive business that spans more than 1,000 locations with over 5,000 team members across Australia, New Zealand and Thailand. We are passionate about our team, with safety and inclusivity at the forefront of everything we do. We’re also passionate about our customers, products, and living our values of:

Doing the right thing
Being in it together
Giving a damn
Getting it done
Job Description

Bapcor is seeking a Senior Business Analyst to support a broad portfolio of business and technology change initiatives across a complex, multi‑brand retail and distribution environment.

This role is available on a full‑time or part‑time basis for the right candidate and is best suited to a mid‑career Business Analyst who enjoys working through ambiguity, understanding how the business operates end‑to‑end, and translating that into practical, deliverable solutions.

While project experience is important, this is not a purely “project BA” role — it’s hands‑on, process‑driven, and spans discovery through to delivery and BAU transition.

You’ll work across multiple initiatives, partnering closely with business stakeholders, product, and delivery teams to shape outcomes that improve how Bapcor operates at scale.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Leading requirements elicitation across complex initiatives, including workshops and stakeholder interviews
Analysing and documenting business requirements with clear traceability to outcomes
Designing and improving end‑to‑end business processes across systems and teams
Supporting solution design through process and data analysis
Collaborating with Product Owners, Delivery Leads, vendors, and technology teams
Identifying risks, dependencies, and change impacts associated with business change
Supporting QA activities, including validation, defect triage, and business sign‑off
Contributing to the development and maturity of the Business Analyst practice
Providing guidance and review support to other Business Analysts
Supporting readiness and transition to BAU to ensure successful adoption
Qualifications

About You

You’re a confident, pragmatic Business Analyst who enjoys figuring things out, even when the environment is complex and not fully defined.

You bring:

Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability
A genuine interest in process, optimisation, and how work gets done
The ability to operate effectively in complex environments
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Confidence influencing outcomes and working across multiple initiatives
A delivery‑focused mindset with attention to detail and quality

Skills & Experience

5–10+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst
Experience supporting complex initiatives or transformation work
Strong requirements elicitation and documentation skills
Business process modelling and analysis experience
Understanding of SDLC and delivery methodologies (Agile/hybrid)
Experience supporting testing and business validation
Ability to provide guidance to less experienced Business Analysts
Tertiary qualifications in Business Analysis, IT or related discipline (preferred)
Agile certification (desirable)
Additional Information

Why Bapcor?

Bapcor is an ASX‑listed organisation with a national footprint of over 1,000 locations and 5,000 team members across Australia, New Zealand, and Thailand.

You’ll gain:

Exposure to a wide variety of initiatives, not just one long‑term project
The ability to see your work drive real operational outcomes
Flexibility with full‑time or part‑time arrangements
A collaborative, supportive culture that values practical delivery
Hybrid working and strong employee benefits, including paid birthday leave and wellbeing support
Career development opportunities across a large, diverse enterprise

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

As a Data Scientist with Optus on a 12 month fixed term contract, you will be at the forefront of driving the development and adoption of innovative AI, machine learning, and advanced analytics. This is an outstanding opportunity to join a team that celebrates innovation, entrepreneurial thinking, and collaboration. You'll play a key role in crafting the future of AI at Optus, delivering tangible business impact while advancing responsible AI practices across the organisation.

What You'll Do | Your day to day will consist of

Design, develop and deploy production grade AI solutions spanning the full spectrum from exploratory analysis and statistical modelling to machine learning and Agentic systems.

Support planning, development, and execution of the Optus AI roadmap across the enterprise, translating strategic objectives into initiatives that drive business outcomes.

Collaborate with business users and partners to develop, test, and detail new ways to help provide data-driven outcomes to the business.

Champion Data Science and support the ongoing implementation of innovative machine learning solutions within the team.

What You Bring | What makes you perfect for the role?

Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g. Data Science, Statistics, Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, Science, or IT).

3+ years of experience in advanced analytics, ML/AI, deep learning, or STEM with practical solution development experience.

Experience using cloud platforms (GCP and/or Azure) along with expertise in modern techniques (Deep Learning, Transformers, etc.) and frameworks (Pytorch, PySpark, LangChain, PyDantic AI, ADK).

Proven experience delivering solutions end-to-end, both independently and collaboratively within cross-functional teams.

Passion for emerging AI technologies and a growth mindset toward continuous learning and experimentation.

The good stuff…. | What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and colleague discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and outstanding shopping discounts with our retail partners.

Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.

Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym, and on-site childcare centre.

Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.

We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.

Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.

Build meaningful connections through colleague-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network, and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).

Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

Free Optus bus from Macquarie University Metro Station (every 6-8 minutes); morning and afternoon.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170.

For more information on Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging at Optus, please visit https://www.optus.com.au/about/inclusion-diversity

About Us

Founded nearly 40 years ago, Micromine is at the cutting edge of next-generation technology for mining. With offices around the world, we are one of the world's premier mining software companies; our technology is used in all corners of the globe.

 

What We Offer

An established multinational business, growing fast, with great benefits:

High growth company providing growth opportunities for our people
Work from home (WFH) and flexibility options to balance work and home life
12 weeks paid parental leave – primary and secondary carers
Free gym membership
Employee Assistance Program for you and your family
Social Connection and Responsibility Program

The opportunity to work across both the Mining and Technology sectors
An engaging team culture and a modern office within Perth’s CBD

 

About The Opportunity

This Commercial Analyst role is a 12-month maternity leave cover and plays a critical part in driving commercial performance across the business.

You will partner closely with Sales, Finance and senior leadership to deliver high-quality analysis, manage incentive frameworks, and provide actionable insights that improve sales effectiveness and long-term customer value.

With a strong focus on advanced Excel modelling and Power BI reporting, this role is ideal for someone who enjoys translating complex data into clear, commercially meaningful insights.

Own reporting and analytics for Sales incentive (OTE) frameworks, including model development and performance tracking
Support quota setting and planning cycles through scenario modelling and analysis
Develop and maintain sales productivity and performance models
Analyse sales performance across regions, products and channels to identify trends and opportunities
Build and enhance customer lifetime value (CLTV), churn and revenue movement models
Deliver regular reporting to Sales Leadership, SLT and Board stakeholders
Partner with Sales Operations and Finance to improve commercial processes and incentive design
Develop and maintain dashboards and reporting in Power BIs




About You

You are a commercially minded analyst who enjoys solving complex problems and working with data to influence decision-making.

Experience in a commercial, finance, or consulting environment
Advanced Excel skills, with strong modelling and scenario analysis capability
Experience working with data visualisation tools (Power BI preferred)
Ability to translate data into actionable business insights

Next Steps

If you are looking to shape the future direction of technology in the industry, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.

To learn more or apply, visit our website at www.micromine.com.

Micromine is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment, and we welcome applicants from a variety of backgrounds to apply.

Your New Role

The Senior Capability Consultant is responsible for the development and execution of learning initiatives that support an uplift in the priority capabilities of colleagues at the Fund. This role will scope, design, develop and implement learning solutions to meet business objectives and align with key priorities.

This role will partner with Capability Leads on uplifting priority skills across the Fund. This includes a primary focus on working with the Capability Lead, Technology & Data.

Key responsibilities include;


Implement development initiatives across the Fund to build AI, data and technical capability.

Design and implement highly effective, results driven colleague development.

Analysis, design, development, implementation, and evaluation of capability development programs delivered online, face to face, virtual or blended.

Facilitation of workshops and learning activities such as learning circles and communities of practice to support continuous learning across the Fund.

Design and develop continuous learning pathways and performance support tools.

Curate and market learning initiatives using a range of resources including LinkedIn Learning and internally developed content.

Build and develop effective relationships with all levels of the Fund including key stakeholders, people leaders, subject matter experts and colleagues.

Ensure learning needs are accurately identified, and recommendations are appropriate to the learning need using effective development principles.

Develop detailed communication and change management plans to support high impact learning and development solutions.



Due to the Fund's international presence, there can be a requirement to work outside of normal business hours.



What You'll Need

Experience in technology and/or data capability development, applying adult learning principles at scale.

Excellent facilitation skills that engage audiences and enhance the transfer of learning.

Experience creating learning programs from analysis to evaluation.

Proven skills in instructional design for digital, blended and face to face delivery.

Strong project management skills including scoping, and identification of key milestones and deliverables.

Well-developed written and oral communication/ interpersonal skills with the ability to present ideas, perspectives, and issues to senior management.

Extensive experience in working under pressure, setting and managing conflicting priorities and meeting specified timeframes.

Understanding of instructional design methodologies such as ADDIE.

Experience in the use of an LMS to monitor and manage learning content and records such as Oracle.

Experience with design software such as RISE and Storyline 360 (Articulate).

Your New Role

We have an opportunity for a Senior Analyst, Operations Change (Business Integration) to join our Investment Operations team.

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Operations Change, this role plays a key part in supporting the delivery of new investments and strategic initiatives into Investment Operations. You'll work across public markets, partnering closely with internal teams and external service providers to ensure operational readiness, strong governance and smooth transitions into BAU.

In This Role, You Will:

Support and lead the implementation of new investment products and strategic initiatives into Investment Operations.
Manage portfolio maintenance activities aligned to fund structures and operational efficiency objectives.
Ensure new investments are operationally set up for governance, management and reporting requirements.
Prepare and review financial instructions, runbooks and operational documentation to support successful delivery.
Drive continuous improvement initiatives to reduce manual processes and support scalable operations.
Build strong, collaborative relationships with internal stakeholders and external service providers.
Contribute to risk, incident and control discussions, supporting a strong risk culture across Operations.




What You'll Need:

Tertiary qualification in Business, Accounting, Finance or a related discipline.
5-7 years' experience in investment operations or a closely related financial services role.
Strong understanding of investment operations across public and private markets.
Experience working with custodians, fund accounting, middle office or trade operations (desirable).
Proven ability to work effectively in change and non‑BAU environments.
Strong analytical, organisational and stakeholder engagement skills.
Ability to work autonomously while contributing to a collaborative, high‑performing team.

We are seeking two experienced BI Specialists to join a high-performing Insights and Analytics function supporting large-scale enterprise transformation and migration programs.

These roles sit within a Business Intelligence practice focused on delivering trusted data, decision support, and high-quality reporting solutions used by senior stakeholders across the business.

This is an excellent opportunity for BI professionals who combine strong technical capability with excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.

About the Role

As a BI Specialist, you will sit on top of an established data asset and focus on information provision and decision support, translating complex data into clear, consumable insights for business users.

You will be responsible for building intuitive Power BI reporting solutions and working closely with non-technical stakeholders to help guide decision-making.

Key Responsibilities



Build and maintain Power BI dashboards and front-end reporting solutions
Develop strong DAX measures and data models
Use SQL and Python to aggregate, analyse and prepare data outputs
Deliver clear, actionable insights to business stakeholders
Present complex data concepts in an easy-to-understand way
Provide support to business users and act as a key point of contact for reporting questions
Contribute to continuous improvement of BI capability and reporting standards
Explore and support AI / Copilot integration where applicable

Skills and Experience Required



Proven experience in a Business Intelligence / BI Specialist / BI Developer role
Strong Power BI development skills (or equivalent enterprise BI tooling)
Advanced DAX capability
Solid SQL and Python skills
Background within Finance or Financial Services (essential)
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
Ability to work closely with business users and explain technical concepts clearly

Highly Regarded



Experience integrating BI solutions with Microsoft Copilot or AI-enabled tooling
Exposure to enterprise BI / BAU data production environments
Experience with large-scale transformation or migration programs

Contract Details



Initial 12-month contract
Strong likelihood of extension to 24 months
Potential pathway to permanent employment for high performers
Two-stage interview process plus informal final discussion

Why This Opportunity?



Join a growing BI practice with multiple upcoming roles
Work directly with senior analytics leaders
Highly visible, stakeholder-facing work
Opportunity to shape how business intelligence supports key programs

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Bespoke Education System Upgrade
Ad Text:
We are partnering with a high-profile NSW Government department supporting a critical uplift of a bespoke Digital Performance & Development system (Digital PDP) 
This is a pure Business Analyst role focused on requirements gathering, stakeholder engagement, and system enhancement, not a delivery or technical configuration role.
You’ll work across a unique environment where:

Schools use a custom-built internal platform
Corporate functions operate on established HR systems like SAP SuccessFactors
The goal is to enhance capability, performance tracking, and user experience across the education system
Role Description
This role sits at the intersection of business, education, and technology.
You will engage directly with schools, operational stakeholders, and internal teams to gather, define, and translate requirements for system upgrades and enhancements.
This is ideal for a BA who is strong in discovery, stakeholder engagement, and working with bespoke/in-house systems.
Key Responsibilities
Lead requirements elicitation through workshops, interviews, and stakeholder engagement
Work closely with schools and non-technical users to capture business needs
Document business and functional requirements (BRDs, user stories, process maps)
Analyse current vs future state processes and identify improvement opportunities
Collaborate with internal technical teams to support solution design and delivery
Ensure alignment between business needs and system capabilities
Support testing, validation, and rollout of enhancements
Key Requirements
Proven experience as a Business Analyst in complex environments
Strong requirements gathering and stakeholder engagement capability
Experience working on bespoke / in-house / custom-built systems (highly preferred)
Ability to work with non-technical stakeholders and translate business needs into clear requirements
Experience in government, education, or large enterprise environments (highly regarded)
Exposure to HR, performance, capability, or learning systems (advantageous)
Strong documentation skills across BRDs, process maps, and user stories
Why This Role
Work on a high-impact system used across schools statewide
Opportunity to shape and enhance a bespoke platform, not just configure off-the-shelf tools
Strong pipeline of work with long-term extension potential
Collaborative, purpose-driven environment within government
Consultant: Sam Baidya

Keywords: Business Analyst, Requirements Gathering, Stakeholder Engagement, Education,  NSW Government, Digital PDP, HR Systems, Performance Management, Learning Systems, Bespoke Systems, Custom Platforms, Process Mapping, BRD, User Stories, Functional Requirements

What You’ll Be Doing

 Solution Architecture Development

Assess current state and define target data architecture for operational and analytical applications.
Collaborate with project, product, and engineering teams to gather requirements and iteratively design solution architecture.
Translate business needs into detailed design specifications aligned with requirements.
Create and maintain clear data architecture designs, patterns, and standards.
Implement observability and controls for data accuracy, consistency, and completeness.
Ensure best practices for data integration across APIs, cloud apps, object storage, data warehouses, and external systems.
Leverage modern technologies: microservices, orchestration, elasticity, observability, and integration patterns.

IT Strategy and Planning

Support adoption and growth of the cloud enterprise data platform.
Contribute to enterprise architecture roadmap and technology evaluations.
Review concept briefs, business cases, and solution specifications for compliance with standards.
Promote standardisation and consolidation across IT landscape.
Define target architectures and migration roadmaps; present to governance forums.

Enterprise Architecture & Governance

Apply industry-standard design and integration patterns.
Collaborate with architects to define strategic direction.
Enforce data governance policies on security, privacy, and metadata management.
Develop and maintain policies, standards, and guidelines for IT selection and implementation.
Advocate EA principles and communicate benefits to stakeholders.

Communication & Team Collaboration

Guide development teams on implementing recommended architectures.
Resolve technology roadblocks and propose alternative solutions.
Design scalable, flexible, and adaptable solutions.
Build understanding of business operations and KPIs to align technology deliverables.
Collaborate with product owners, analysts, engineers, and scientists to enable data-driven decisions.
Foster team building, knowledge sharing, and skills development.

Risk & Compliance

Assist in identifying and assessing operational, financial, and reputational risks.
Contribute to risk management frameworks, policies, and controls.
Liaise with security and audit teams to ensure solution integrity and robustness.

What’s In It For You

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours. We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

Qualifications

Degree (or equivalent) in an IT-related field required.
AWS Associate Architect or other cloud certification highly regarded.
Formal architecture certification (e.g., TOGAF) is an advantage.

Experience

7+ years in Information Technology, including minimum 2 years as a Data Architect.
Strong interpersonal skills with ability to build consensus across multi-disciplinary teams.

Advanced skills in SQL, Python, and Spark (highly desirable).
Expertise in dimensional modelling and 3rd normal form modelling.
Hands-on experience with one or more platforms:
Databricks, AWS data services (S3, DMS, RDS, DynamoDB, Redshift), Power BI, Tableau, IBM Cognos, IBM TM1.
Knowledge of machine learning and AI (desirable).
Industry experience in comparator, digital marketing, insurance, or financial services (desirable).

About the Role An exciting opportunity exists for a commercially minded Financial Modelling professional to join a high-performing advisory team delivering pricing and cost recovery models across a large, complex environment.

This role sits within a Commercial Evaluation and Advisory function and focuses on developing, refining, and implementing pricing models that support strategic decision-making and whole-of-organisation service delivery.

You’ll take ownership of financial models, working across both new and existing projects—sometimes building from scratch, other times improving and optimising established frameworks.

Commercial Pricing / Financial Modelling Analyst (Contract)
Parramatta / Hybrid (Initial onsite training, then flexible WFH)
$1,100 per day + super
Contract to 3 November 2026 - Extension Likely
40 hours per week

Key Responsibilities
Design, build and enhance financial and commercial pricing models, including scenario analysis
Support business cases and funding submissions with robust, consistent modelling outputs
Develop Excel-based cost models to support production in IBM Planning Analytics (TM1)
Identify and integrate external data sources into modelling frameworks
Partner with Finance and business stakeholders to guide activity-based costing approaches
Analyse cost structures and support sustainable pricing strategies across multiple service lines
About You You’re a proactive, self-driven analyst who enjoys solving complex problems and working with ambiguity. You combine strong technical modelling skills with commercial thinking and stakeholder engagement.
You will bring:

Strong experience in financial modelling (advanced Excel essential)
Exposure to pricing, cost recovery, or commercial analysis
Ability to build models from scratch and refine existing ones
Experience working with large, complex datasets and multiple inputs
Confidence engaging with senior stakeholders and navigating differing viewpoints
A solutions-focused mindset with the ability to manage competing priorities
Highly regarded:

Exposure to IBM Planning Analytics (TM1) or similar tools
Experience in government, infrastructure, or large-scale organisations
Interest or experience in automation (e.g. Python, process improvement, data integration)
Key Challenges
Developing sustainable pricing models across multiple divisions and cost centres
Managing competing deadlines and project interdependencies
Influencing and aligning diverse stakeholders with varying priorities
Why Apply?
Join a highly experienced, collaborative and supportive team
Work on impactful, high-visibility projects
Flexible hybrid working environment (2 days in office)

Optus is a leading Australian telecommunications provider, delivering more than 11 million services every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

We're seeking a Risk Governance and Performance Manager to strengthen risk, controls, and compliance across our Consumer Business Unit. Working at the intersection of data, risk, and decision‑making, you'll translate complex information into clear insights that support leaders, protect customers, and drive effective frontline execution.

With end‑to‑end oversight of Consumer risk and compliance, you'll partner with senior leaders, risk and compliance teams, and analytics to identify trends, surface emerging risks, and support governance forums. This is an ideal role for someone who enjoys working with data, influencing stakeholders, and connecting risk frameworks to real customer, regulatory, and business outcomes.

What You'll Do

Create a consistent approach to governance within the Line 1 Risk & Controls team and play a lead role in preparing materials for Consumer Business unit governance forums and underlying business profile risk forums
Put that approach into practice, collaborating with other members of the line 1 risk team and business partners to assemble governance materials that provide timely and accurate information to enable oversight and decision making.
Design and deliver automated reporting tools and dashboard that provides clear visibility of controls performance, issues, incidents, risks and compliance obligations, enabling day to day management of risk as well as good governance outcomes across Consumer.
Establish and track key risk indicators (KRIs), control metrics, and leading indicators aligned to risk appetite.
Analyse data to identify control gaps, systemic issues, and emerging risks, escalating material findings through governance forums.
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling insights to support senior leadership decision‑making.
Partner with Line 1, Line 2, analytics, IT, and risk teams to support assurance activities, control uplift initiatives, and reporting automation.
Act as a trusted advisor to Consumer leaders on control effectiveness and risk performance, while championing a strong risk‑based culture.

What makes you perfect

5+ years' experience in governance, audit, controls performance, risk, compliance, assurance, quality, or insights‑led roles—ideally in a regulated, customer‑facing environment.
Demonstrated experience preparing written materials for senior governance forums related to risk and compliance matters
Strong understanding of risk and control frameworks, governance, and regulatory obligations.
Demonstrated experience delivering meaningful reporting, analytics, or assurance outcomes for senior stakeholders.
Strong analytical capability, with the ability to turn data into practical, outcome‑focused insights.
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence influencing across multiple functions, and experience in coaching others to improve outcomes.
A pragmatic, customer‑centric approach to risk—focused on improving outcomes, not checklist compliance.

What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.
Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.
Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.
Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170. We're here to help. When you contact us, please advise your preferred method of contact and we will be in touch within 48 hours.

For more information on Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging at Optus, please visit https://www.optus.com.au/about/inclusion-diversity.

Why Ubank

Job Title: Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst - Fund Accounting
Ad Text:
About the Company
Davidson is partnering with a well-regarded organisation within the superannuation sector to appoint an experienced Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst on a 12-month fixed term contract. This is an exciting opportunity to join a business delivering important change across investment operations, finance and technology, with a strong focus on fund accounting and unit pricing environments.

About the Role
The Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst will play a pivotal role in leading and shaping complex initiatives across fund accounting, unit pricing and broader investment operations. Sitting at the intersection of business, finance and technology, this role will be responsible for driving delivery outcomes while also leading business analysis activities to ensure requirements are clearly defined, agreed and translated into effective solutions.

This role will suit someone who can move comfortably between strategic stakeholder engagement, detailed business analysis and end-to-end project delivery.

Key responsibilities will include:

Leading end-to-end delivery of initiatives across fund accounting, unit pricing and investment operations
Partnering with business, finance and technology stakeholders to identify needs, define requirements and deliver fit-for-purpose solutions
Leading business analysis activities including requirement gathering, process mapping, impact assessments and documentation
Managing project plans, governance, risks, issues, dependencies and reporting across multiple workstreams
Translating business requirements into clear delivery plans and actionable outcomes in collaboration with technical teams
Driving workshops and stakeholder discussions to build alignment, clarify priorities and support decision-making
Supporting system and process improvements across investment and funds management operations
Coordinating internal teams and external vendors to ensure successful delivery and implementation outcomes
Acting as the key link between business stakeholders and delivery teams to ensure solutions align with operational and strategic objectives

About You
The ideal candidate will bring a blend of project management and lead business analysis capability, with a strong track record of delivering complex change within superannuation, investment operations or broader financial services environments.

You will bring:

Proven experience in a Project Manager, Lead Business Analyst or hybrid delivery role within financial services
Strong experience working across fund accounting and unit pricing environments
Deep understanding of superannuation, investments or funds management operations
Demonstrated ability to lead both project delivery and business analysis activities across finance and technology functions
Strong capability in requirements gathering, process analysis, workshop facilitation and stakeholder engagement
Experience working on system-led change initiatives in partnership with technology teams
Strong governance, planning, risk and issue management capability
Experience engaging with external vendors, implementation partners or service providers
Excellent communication, negotiation and relationship management skills
The ability to operate confidently in a fast-paced, evolving and highly regulated environment

The Benefits

Opportunity to contribute to a high-profile multi-year program
12-month fixed term contract with meaningful, business-critical project scope
Hybrid working arrangements with 2 days per week in the office or 8 days per month
Flexibility to be based in either Sydney or Melbourne


Please apply with current resume in Microsoft Word format only (.doc or .docx). If you have the relevant experience above and require further information, please contact Tara Gearon at tara.gearon@davidsonwp.com quoting reference JN -042026-42915. Want to know more about Davidson? Visit us at www.davidsonwp.com

The Company
Our client is a large ASX‑listed organisation undergoing a significant period of growth, portfolio optimisation and strategic asset activity. With a strong pipeline of acquisitions, divestments and major capital projects, they are seeking a commercially minded Finance Analyst to join their high‑performing corporate finance team. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a fast‑paced, deal‑driven environment and brings experience from projects, engineering, construction, infrastructure or other asset‑intensive industries.

The Role
Reporting to the CFO and working close with the Corporate Finance Manager, you will play a key role in supporting the organisation’s M&A activity, asset transactions and strategic investment decisions. Your work will directly influence executive‑level decision making and long‑term portfolio strategy. Some of your duties will include:

Financial analysis and modelling for acquisitions, divestments and capital projects
Supporting due diligence across financial, operational and commercial workstreams
Preparing valuation models, scenario analysis and investment papers
Supporting the month‑end and reporting process for M&A‑related accounting impacts
About You
You will be an experienced Finance Analyst who has worked in due diligence and M&A environments who is able to independently work on analysis of financials and also create scenario based models. Ideally you would have started your career in Big 4 or in a M&A consulting environment.

To Apply
If you are interested in discussing please apply through this advert or email Simon Clancy at sclancy@fourquarters.com.au

Ad Text:
Passionate about education?

Want to help improve outcomes for schools and students?

Reviews and School Improvement (RSI) provides system monitoring and assurance to support continuous improvement in Queensland state schools and education centres by providing leadership and oversight for targeted insights reviews. In addition, the RSI contributes significantly to distilling and sharing evidence-based insights and effective practices, to inform school improvement across the Queensland education system. Insights reviews (behaviour) will provide schools with practical guidance to strengthen their discipline systems and practices, enabling every school to deliver excellence through maintaining high expectations, a positive learning culture and quality teaching and learning.

The successful candidate will be employed at the classification listed on the attached role description under EOSD provisions.

About the Role

RSI are seeking Principals, Insights Reviews (behaviour) with particular knowledge and experience in leading effective and responsive school systems and practices which support student engagement for learning and providing effective support and interventions for student with diverse learning needs and complex behaviours.

As the Principal, Insights Reviews you will:

Lead insights reviews (behaviour) across Queensland State Schools to inform school and system improvement.
Play a key role in the delivery of the insights reviews as a team member.
Contribute to development of insights reviews methodology and processes.

Responsibilities include:

Deliver insights reviews, aligned to system priorities and initiatives, using evidence-based tools and methodologies.
Conduct insights reviews in line with agreed methodologies and processes.
As chair, lead teams, including the support and mentoring of review team members.
Capture high-quality evidence in reports that generate insights to inform school, region and system decision making.
Identify and share effective practice.
Collaboratively develop and implement approaches responsive to current and emerging system priorities.
Foster high quality collaboration, communication, cultural awareness and information sharing to enable an understanding of the complexities and challenges that exist in specific targeted areas.
Develop a rapport and engage with a range of external and internal stakeholders including Principals, school leaders, teachers, school staff, students, parents, regional staff and carers and community members to effectively and collaboratively enable an integrated approach to deliver the vision of the department.
Provide advice and expertise to improve school systems and processes that promote engagement and educational outcomes for all students, including those with diverse and/or complex learning needs.
Understand and apply the legislation and policies that influence schooling especially in relation to students with diverse and/or complex learning needs and young people who are not actively engaged in a learning pathway.
Develop, articulate and promote a shared educational vision focused on providing quality learning outcomes for all students, including those with diverse and/or complex learning needs.

The timing and location of school reviews can be a determining factor in when the employee is appointed.

Successful applicants will be expected to attend training prior to undertaking the role.

Applicants must be available to travel, including overnight absences.

Mandatory Requirements:

You must be a permanent employee of the department with a substantive teaching or classified officer role employed under Teaching in State Education Award - State 2016, have current full registration as a teacher and should be aware that temporary internal moves for the purposes of the department's payroll processes are referred to as internal secondments
You must be currently employed as a Principal or Acting Principal in a Queensland state school
Employees engaged temporarily as Education Officer - Special Duties have a requirement of 36¼ hours per week attendance and arrangements as outlined in clause 7.4 of the Department of Education State School Teachers Certified Agreement 2019

Interested? To Apply: 

Attach a brief resume (maximum 2 pages) including contact details for 2 referees (referees should have an understanding of your relevant work history)
Attach a maximum two-page response demonstrating your experiences in leading a school improvement agenda, utilising strong interpersonal skills to be an effective leader, and your suitability for the role
Completion of the Principal, Insights Reviews questionnaire (Please note: once you have submitted your application documents and pressed next, you will be prompted to answer the required questionnaire)

We welcome all applicants to share any support needed to ensure our recruitment process is inclusive.

Applications remain current for 12 months from the closing date and may be considered for appointment to identical or similar vacancies within the Department.

Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

Your new opportunity
Queensland Treasury plays a central role in supporting the Government's infrastructure and investment priorities. We are seeking a Principal Commercial Analyst to join our Commercial Project Advisory team within the Infrastructure Projects group.

In this role, you will work on complex and high-profile commercial matters, supporting government engagement with major infrastructure projects and private sector partners. You will contribute to practical, well-considered advice that informs investment decisions and delivery approaches across government, helping ensure projects are affordable, viable and deliver long-term value for Queenslanders.

This is an opportunity to apply your commercial expertise in a trusted central agency environment, where your work will directly influence outcomes of state-wide significance.

In this role, you will:
• Support the delivery of high-quality commercial, financial and transaction advice to government agencies and Treasury, particularly in relation to investment decisions, business cases, procurement and delivery models.
• Contribute to the development and assessment of delivery strategies, including Public Private Partnerships and other commercial models, by analysing risks, market capability and commercial structures.
• Analyse financial and commercial documentation such as feasibility assessments, financial models, cost estimates and demand forecasts to support robust, evidence-based recommendations.
• Support commercial assurance activities by constructively challenging assumptions, methodologies and commercial positions to strengthen project proposals.
• Build and manage effective working relationships with stakeholders across government and industry to support market engagement, negotiations and coordinated advice.
• Assist in resolving complex issues by contributing to timely, practical and outcome-focused solutions, including leading or supporting responses when issues arise.
• Build strong networks across government and industry to support successful project outcomes.

What experience you will bring:
• Demonstrated commercial experience in infrastructure, investment or major project environments, with a strong focus on value for money, affordability and appropriate risk allocation.
• Strong commercial acumen within a government, regulatory or policy context.
• Highly developed stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills, with the ability to work effectively across government, central agencies and industry to support coordinated, outcome-focused advice.
• The ability to apply commercial judgement in a government setting, synthesising financial, economic and strategic inputs to support sound investment decisions.

Whilst there are no mandatory qualifications needed for this role, relevant qualifications will be highly regarded.

How to apply
To submit your application, click on the Apply link to provide:
• Your resume detailing relevant experience
• A cover letter of no more than two pages outlining why you are interested in this position and how your knowledge, skills and experience makes you a strong candidate for the role.

Queensland Treasury is committed to building an inclusive and diverse workforce that reflects the community we serve and treating all applicants equitably. This starts with recruitment, including making reasonable adjustments to support applicants at all stages of the process.
